title: Deep Learning-Based Analysis of a Real-Time  Voice Cloning System
author: Ndjoli Isaaka Luc Emmanuel
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Voice technology has emerged as a hotspot for 
deep learning research due to fast advancements in 
computer technology. The goal of human-computer 
interaction should be to give computers the ability to feel, 
see, hear, and speak. Voice is the most favorable 
approach for future interactions between humans and 
computers because it offers more benefits than any other 
method. One example of voice technology that is capable 
of imitating a particular person's voice is voice cloning. 
Real-time voice cloning with only a few samples is 
proposed as a solution to the issue of having to provide a 
large amount of samples and having to endure a long time 
in the past for voice cloning. This strategy deviates from 
the conventional model. 
For independent training, different databases and 
models are used but for joint modeling, only three models 
are used. The vocoder makes use of a novel type of 

 LPCNET that works well on certain samples and low- 
performance devices.
steps_raw:
Voice technology has emerged as a hotspot for 
deep learning research due to fast advancements in 
computer technology. The goal of human-computer 
interaction should be to give computers the ability to feel, 
see, hear, and speak. Voice is the most favorable 
approach for future interactions between humans and 
computers because it offers more benefits than any other 
method. One example of voice technology that is capable 
of imitating a particular person's voice is voice cloning. 
Real-time voice cloning with only a few samples is 
proposed as a solution to the issue of having to provide a 
large amount of samples and having to endure a long time 
in the past for voice cloning. This strategy deviates from 
the conventional model. 
For independent training, different databases and 
models are used but for joint modeling, only three models 
are used. The vocoder makes use of a novel type of 

 LPCNET that works well on certain samples and low- 
performance devices.
